description: Create a first-party workflow integration module registry for declarative module metadata.
Support module metadata fields for group key, label, description, tile kind, icon token, default action ID, action IDs, and availability key.
Allow designer catalog construction to include registered first-party integration modules as stable app records.
Preserve existing built-in action grouping behavior for core, transform, and AI modules.
Preserve existing extension app filtering behavior when adding first-party integration filtering.
Add an availability resolver mechanism keyed by declarative availability keys.
Implement the NinjaOne availability resolver using active rmm_integrations rows for the current tenant.
Filter the workflow designer catalog so the NinjaOne module is returned only when NinjaOne is active for the tenant.
Register a NinjaOne workflow module with group key app:ninjaone, NinjaOne label, NinjaOne icon token, and default action.
Map the NinjaOne icon token in the Workflow Designer palette so the tile is visibly NinjaOne-specific.
Register the ninjaone.devices.find workflow action with schema, metadata, and handler.
Implement NinjaOne device find/list output with normalized device fields suitable for downstream mappings.
Register the ninjaone.devices.sync workflow action with schema, metadata, and handler.
Implement single-device sync by reusing existing NinjaOne sync engine behavior.
Register the ninjaone.devices.reboot workflow action with side-effectful metadata and engine-provided idempotency.
Implement NinjaOne device reboot by validating tenant/integration/device context and calling the existing NinjaOne client.
Register the ninjaone.alerts.list_active workflow action with schema, metadata, and handler.
Implement active alert listing with normalized alert outputs for downstream Ticket module mappings.
prdRefs: Primary flow: automate from NinjaOne alert to generic ticket creation

description: Register the ninjaone.alerts.get workflow action with schema, metadata, and handler.
Implement get-alert behavior by external alert ID or alert UID and return normalized alert fields.
Register the ninjaone.alerts.reset workflow action with side-effectful metadata and engine-provided idempotency.
Display ninjaone.alerts.reset as Acknowledge alert in the designer while keeping the technical ID stable.
Implement acknowledge-alert behavior by calling the NinjaOne reset alert operation after tenant/integration validation.
Ensure all NinjaOne workflow action handlers fail fast when tenant context is missing.
Ensure all NinjaOne workflow action handlers fail fast when NinjaOne is inactive for the tenant.
Ensure all NinjaOne workflow action handlers avoid returning credentials or sensitive secret-derived data.
Register NinjaOne workflow actions in the server runtime used by catalog and publish validation.
Register NinjaOne workflow actions in the Temporal worker runtime used for action execution.
Expose NinjaOne action input and output schemas through the existing workflow registry/catalog endpoints.
Ensure the NinjaOne designer module action dropdown contains only the approved NinjaOne actions.
Ensure NinjaOne alert outputs include fields needed to map into generic ticket creation.
prdRefs: Primary flow: automate from NinjaOne alert to generic ticket creation

description: Keep ticket creation out of the NinjaOne module and rely on existing Ticket actions for that flow.
Use clear user-facing action labels and descriptions for all NinjaOne actions.
Document the module registry pattern enough for future first-party integrations to adopt it.
